title: AI-Powered Self-Service & Agent Assist Expansion
description: This blueprint expands on the existing AI-Assisted Support Ticketing project by adding a customer-facing self-service portal and enhancing agent-facing tools. It aims to deflect repetitive questions entirely through a knowledge base and provide real-time, context-aware assistance to agents for complex queries.

prototypePreview.title: Prototype concept
prototypePreview.summary: This blueprint expands on the existing AI-Assisted Support Ticketing project by adding a customer-facing self-service portal and enhancing agent-facing tools.

architecture: User Interface: Customer Self-Service Portal (React, Next.js)
API Gateway: Secure API for external & internal access (AWS API Gateway)
Orchestration Layer: Intelligent Routing & Workflow Engine (Meridian Workflow Engine)
AI Reasoning Layer: LLM & Vector Database for context (OpenAI GPT-4, Pinecone/Weaviate)
Knowledge Management: Centralized Knowledge Base (Confluence, Internal DB)
Integration Layer: Ticketing System & Data Sources Connectors (Meridian Connect, Existing Ticketing Platform)
Data & Observability: Usage Analytics & Performance Monitoring (Snowflake, Datadog)

conflicts.description: Engineering sequencing overlaps with the infrastructure migration project, risking shared team contention in Q2-Q3.
conflicts.resolution: Phase foundation work after the migration milestone in August. Shared infrastructure team rotates in Q4.

conflicts.description: Running the first two blueprints in parallel would duplicate platform spend and exceed the Q2 remaining budget envelope of $3.3M.
conflicts.resolution: Share core platform services and consolidate vendor contracts. Sequence capex spend across quarters.

scoringInsights.summary: The estimated CAPEX of $350k is well within the remaining $850k CAPEX budget and the $3.3M total remaining budget.
Builds on 'AI-Assisted Support Ticketing' but requires additional engineering focus, which is already 87% allocated.
Leverages the Meridian Workflow Engine and Meridian Connect for deeper automation, enhancing existing product value.
Aligns with the approved text-based AI assistant approach, avoiding the privacy issues of rejected voice assistants.
Directly addresses the 'AI-Native Automation' trend and the 'AI decision-assist layer' opportunity with high relevance.
Engineering capacity is high at 87% allocation, and there's a skill gap in AI/ML Engineering, potentially delaying execution.
Requires careful review for GDPR (DPIA, consent) and AI Ethics (human oversight, explainability, bias audit) given AI's role.

timeline.name: Phase 1 — Foundation & Self-Service MVP
deliverables: Finalize proj-001 integration
Basic FAQ chatbot deployment
Knowledge base synchronization

timeline.name: Phase 2 — Advanced Agent Assist & Portal
deliverables: Agent-facing recommendation engine
Dynamic content expansion for self-service portal
User feedback mechanism implementation

timeline.name: Phase 3 — Continuous Improvement & Expansion
deliverables: A/B testing for AI responses
Integration with additional internal data sources
Sentiment analysis for query prioritization

title: Enterprise SaaS AI Customer Engagement Platform
description: This blueprint proposes adopting a leading SaaS platform specializing in AI-driven customer support, providing a comprehensive solution from intelligent chatbots to agent-assist tools. This approach minimizes internal development effort and leverages vendor expertise for rapid deployment and continuous updates.

prototypePreview.title: Prototype concept
prototypePreview.summary: This blueprint proposes adopting a leading SaaS platform specializing in AI-driven customer support, providing a comprehensive solution from intelligent chatbots to agent-assist tools.

architecture: User Interface: Omni-channel Customer Engagement Portal (Vendor SaaS UI)
SaaS Platform Core: AI-powered Chatbot & NLU Engine (Zendesk/ServiceNow AI)
Knowledge Management: Centralized & Dynamic Knowledge Base (Vendor KB)
Integration Layer: CRM & Ticketing System Connectors (Vendor API, Meridian Connect)
Data Sync & ETL: Secure Data Ingestion Pipeline (AWS Glue/Azure Data Factory)
Security & Compliance: Data Encryption & Access Control (Vendor Security Features, SSO)
Analytics & Reporting: Performance & User Behavior Dashboards (Vendor Analytics)

conflicts.description: Engineering sequencing overlaps with the infrastructure migration project, risking shared team contention in Q2-Q3.
conflicts.resolution: Phase foundation work after the migration milestone in August. Shared infrastructure team rotates in Q4.

conflicts.description: Running the first two blueprints in parallel would duplicate platform spend and exceed the Q2 remaining budget envelope of $3.3M.
conflicts.resolution: Share core platform services and consolidate vendor contracts. Sequence capex spend across quarters.

scoringInsights.summary: The estimated CAPEX of $180k is well within the remaining $850k CAPEX budget, and OPEX is manageable within overall spending.
This approach requires minimal direct involvement from the highly allocated Engineering team, freeing up internal resources.
While it integrates, it doesn't directly leverage or enhance core Meridian products, potentially leading to some feature overlap.
Avoids the high-risk, high-cost, and compliance-heavy aspects of previously rejected large-scale internal platform builds.
Aligns with the 'Platform Consolidation' trend and leverages established AI capabilities from market leaders like ServiceNow.
Significantly reduces the burden on internal Engineering and ML teams, aligning well with current high allocation and skill gaps.
Requires thorough vendor assessment for SOC 2, GDPR, and AI Ethics compliance, and legal review of multi-year SaaS contracts.

timeline.name: Phase 1 — Vendor Selection & Core Setup
deliverables: RFP and vendor selection
Contract negotiation and legal review
Initial platform configuration and SSO integration

timeline.name: Phase 2 — Integration & Pilot Launch
deliverables: Integration with existing ticketing/CRM via Meridian Connect
Pilot self-service chatbot for common FAQs
Initial data migration and knowledge base population

timeline.name: Phase 3 — Full Rollout & Optimization
deliverables: Expand to full customer base
Onboard customer support agents to assist tools
Continuous training and performance monitoring

title: Hybrid Knowledge Orchestration & Agent Augmentation
description: This blueprint empowers support agents with an intelligent knowledge orchestration layer, combining internal workflow automation (Meridian Workflow Engine) with a dedicated external AI knowledge management solution. It aims to reduce repetitive agent tasks by providing instant access to relevant information and automated response suggestions.

prototypePreview.title: Prototype concept
prototypePreview.summary: This blueprint empowers support agents with an intelligent knowledge orchestration layer, combining internal workflow automation (Meridian Workflow Engine) with a dedicated external AI knowledge management solution.

architecture: Agent Interface: Unified Agent Desktop (Existing Ticketing System UI + Custom Widget)
Orchestration Layer: Contextual Workflow Automation (Meridian Workflow Engine)
Knowledge AI Layer: External Knowledge Base & NLQ (Coveo, Algolia with AI)
Content Ingestion: Internal & External Data Connectors (Meridian Connect, Custom Scrapers)
Data Preprocessing: Document Parsing & Embedding (Python, SpaCy)
Search & Retrieval: Vector Search Index (Managed Service or open source like Milvus)
Analytics & Feedback: Agent Performance & Knowledge Gap Analysis (Meridian Analytics Hub)

conflicts.description: The third blueprint requires ML engineering capacity currently allocated to the AI ticketing project until July.
conflicts.resolution: Assign after July milestone or bring in contract ML engineer for initial phase.

scoringInsights.summary: The estimated CAPEX of $280k is well within the remaining $850k CAPEX budget and Operations' unallocated contingency.
Requires some Engineering effort for integration and custom development, potentially impacting partially allocated teams like Strategy & Data.
Deeply integrates with and enhances the Meridian Workflow Engine and Meridian Analytics Hub, strengthening the core platform.
Avoids large-scale, high-risk internal builds, focusing instead on augmenting existing systems with targeted external AI.
Addresses the demand for AI decision-assist layers and leverages existing platform strengths, aligning with consolidation trends.
Requires a mix of Engineering, Data, and Operations expertise, which might strain partially allocated resources but less than a full build.
Requires careful data privacy assessment (DPIA) for data shared with external AI KB and ensuring compliance with AI Ethics policies.

timeline.name: Phase 1 — Knowledge Base & Integration MVP
deliverables: Select and integrate external AI knowledge base
Synchronize core knowledge articles
Develop basic agent search widget for ticketing system

timeline.name: Phase 2 — Workflow Augmentation & Smart Suggestions
deliverables: Implement Meridian Workflow for contextual routing
Add AI-powered response suggestions to agents
Establish agent feedback loop for content improvement

timeline.name: Phase 3 — Performance Tuning & Expansion
deliverables: Optimize knowledge retrieval and suggestion accuracy
Expand content sources (e.g., product docs, community forums)
Integrate agent performance analytics into Analytics Hub
